title: Aboriginal Dreamtime Stories
extract: The Aboriginal Australians have the oldest continuing cultural tradition on Earth, stretching back at least 65,000 years. Their Dreamtime stories aren't just myths—they're sophisticated maps of the landscape encoded in narrative. A song about a serpent creating a river isn't metaphor; it's a geological record of water courses that modern hydrologists have confirmed. When Uncle Jimmy took me to country near Kakadu, he pointed to rock art depicting fish species that haven't lived in those waters for 10,000 years. The paintings are scientific records, created by people who observed and documented their world with extraordinary precision long before Europeans arrived.
